measure.note: FLOOR (zero new code): the artifact covers all four sections the skill requires (sketch language, per-cue mark table, climax sketch, composer hand-off). LEAN ARTIFACT concision could otherwise cut a whole section rather than a sentence within it; this catches the omission the non-empty check can't (non-empty only proves the file isn't blank).

FLOOR (zero new code): the CUE-RELATIVE FRAMING RULE's own FORBIDDEN example shape — three bare, consecutive numeric-only table columns with no `cue=` reference (the skill's literal '| 891 | 24 | 1057 |' anti-pattern for a master-timeline absolute). A compliant row always carries a named cue id / cue= token and prose cells (anchor, purpose) between its numeric offsets, so this never false-fires on real cue-relative rows (verified against the real fen-yu-he/sketch-overlay.md, which never trips it).

run.note: THIN WRAPPER (Part D): the two node-specific deterministic invariants a JSON schema can't encode — the restraint BUDGET (marks <= floor(cueCount*0.6), counting '×N' multi-instance rows, not just marked cues) and the MARK VOCABULARY (only underline|wrap-arc|label-arrow|vs-mark). Best-effort: an unreadable/tableless sketch-overlay.md writes parseError:true and exits 0 (the section-presence gate above and the non-empty checks.post gate own that failure). Verified against the real fen-yu-he/sketch-overlay.md (reproduces its own stated ledger: 9 cues, 3 marks, budget 5, no violations) and a synthetic over-budget + off-vocabulary fixture (both flip to true).

measure.note: FLOOR (hard fail): the restraint budget was exceeded — the single most load-bearing numeric rule the sketch-explainer-layer skill states ("Restraint is the rule"). VALIDITY: fires+discriminates (see mark-restraint-lint.mjs's note).
FLOOR (hard fail): a marked row named a mark type outside {underline, wrap-arc, label-arrow, vs-mark} — the skill's closed vocabulary ("NOT in this vocabulary (don't invent)").
